channel.title: Software Testing Weekly
channel.description: Join 9,100+ testers and subscribe for the best Software Testing news in your inbox. Every week — for free!

item.title: 【Software Testing Weekly - Issue 309 - Apr 20th 2026】软件测试周刊 - 第 309 期 - 2026 年 4 月 20 日
item.description:
【

COMMENT

Welcome to the 309th issue!

Looking for a software testing tool?

Check out TestVerse — a free directory of categorised 190+ testing tools by Anshita Bhasin.

And if you want to learn more about how to use it, she wrote a handy guide.

Happy testing! 🙂

Dawid Dylowicz

Sponsored Link

Coding agents Are Shipping 55% Faster. Keep Up at the Agentic Testing Summit.

The outer loop has become the new bottleneck for AI-generated code. Join mabl and QA leaders for a half-day summit to learn how to build an agentic testing strategy, network with peers who are experimenting with new approaches, and leave with a clearer picture of how your team stays ahead. Register for a summit near you.

mabl.com

NEWS

AI Is Changing Software Testing. After 20 Years in QA, I Can Tell You This Shift Is Different.

Olga Manuilova reflects on 20 years in QA and explains how the profession is moving from execution toward quality strategy and risk management.

Additionally, Simon Prior shares some fresh thoughts on Intelligent Quality Leadership: A Model Still Taking Shape.

medium.com

Quality Isn't a Testing Problem

A good reminder from Alan Page that quality lives in the system and it should be built-in, rather than tested-in.

This is followed by a fantastic post from Filip Hric about Maslow's Hammer and Three Lies QA Tells Itself.

substack.com

Rethinking Your Test Strategy for AI-Powered Features

Testing AI features? Irfan Mujagić shares a test strategy, including a practical framework for deciding what to automate, what to check manually and what to monitor.

You can also follow Emily O'Connor and Richard Adams's advice on Two ways to use the "5 whys" method: Root cause of bugs and identifying continuous improvements.

thegreenreport.blog

Testing Is Systems Thinking

A thought-provoking article by Patrick Prill describing how to use the DSRP mental model (Distinctions, Systems, Relationships, Perspectives) for better testing.

Moreover, Patrick also explains why AI Cannot Replace Glue Work.

wordpress.com

You Climbed the Test Pyramid. Now Eat a Custard Slice.

What if you thought about your test suite by the value each layer provides, not just the technical scope? Stuart Crocker introduces the Custard Slice as an alternative to the Test Pyramid.

At the same time, Shubham Sharma came up with The Testing Lattice — a handy way of assessing your testing maturity.

dragonsforelevenses.com

AUTOMATION

How AI Testing Agents Are Transforming QA at Showpad

Looking for advice on how to embed AI agents into daily QA work? Ganeshan Manickam describes how they built small agents for each testing phase and what they learned from that.

medium.com

The Chaos Mutant: What if a Bot Tried to Break Your Code Every Night?

Prateek Singh gives an overview of a nightly mutation testing setup that randomly picks recently changed files, injects bugs and shows which ones your tests would miss.

Also, Thulane Khumalo asks and explains: What are semtests?

medium.com

The 'valuable' in valuable feedback, fast

What makes a good test automation? Bas Dijkstra breaks it down into four angles and shares practical tips, such as keeping the test scope small and using custom assertion messages. Bas also shared My thoughts on 'self-healing' in test automation.

ontestautomation.com

Why Layered Test Framework Architecture Still Matters in the Age of AI

Josphine Job goes through the six layers of a test automation framework and explains why AI doesn't replace them but adds an orchestration layer on top.

Moreover, Mohammad Faisal Khatri shows an example of implementing Builder Pattern + Faker in API Test Automation using Playwright with TypeScript.

medium.com

You Write Too Many Tests

If you don't have confidence in your integration tests, Chris Tonkinson explains why you should probably have fewer of them and use a Scenario Tree Testing pattern for top-down coverage instead.

Similarly, Oliver Fries describes how I stopped treating testing methodologies as religion and started treating them as tools in the context of TDD and BDD.

medium.com

TOOLS

Creating a resilient test framework with the Playwright Page Object Model (POM)

If you want to build a test framework that scales, Shawn Vernier shows a Page Object Model setup with examples in Playwright.

A great additional read to that is Aparna Mishra's take on Playwright POM (Without Classes): Make Your Tests Clean & Reusable.

ministryoftesting.com

Playprom: Turn your Playwright test run into a time-series data

Want to see your Playwright test results over time? Lutfi Fitroh Hadi built Playprom — a reporter that emits test runs as metrics into Prometheus and Grafana.

Similarly, Vitali Haradkou created a handy solution for Playwright test reports that actually look good in email.

medium.com

Playwright Accessibility Testing: What axe and Lighthouse Miss

If you're using automated tools for accessibility testing, you may be interested to learn from David Mello which defects scanners can't catch and how to leverage Playwright for better results.

And if you use Cypress, Gleb Bahmutov has also got you covered: Testing A11y Using Cypress And wick-a11y Plugin.

davidmello.com

Playwright MCP Vision + DevTools: AI Agents That See the Page AND Read Network Traffic Simultaneously

Do you know about Playwright MCP's capability modes? Pramod Dutta gives an overview of snapshot, vision, and devtools with practical examples of when to use each one and how to combine them.

Moreover, Gurudatt S A tells us Why Your Playwright getByRole Locator Isn't Working — Understanding ARIA and Accessible Names.

medium.com

UTML: A UI Test Markup Language for Browser Automation

Kulanthaivelu Sankaran proposes UTML, a JSON-based markup language for browser tests, which you can use to describe steps, such as "click Sign in button", and let it handle the rest. You can also read part 2 and part 3.

medium.com

BOOKS

Reading of The Staff Engineer's Path

This is a thoughtful reflection from Roy Straub after rereading The Staff Engineer's Path now that he's become a Tech Lead and has a different perspective.

rstraub.com

VIDEOS

From Developer to Tester: Navigating the Shift in an AI-Driven Testing World

An insightful, 46-minute discussion between David Burns and Benjamin Bischoff about the evolving role of software testers in the AI-powered world.

Similarly, Alan Richardson invited Krisztián Fischer and Toby Sears to talk about AI and Software Testing.

youtube.com

How to identify risks which matter most for testing

Vitaly Sharovatov started a new series of recordings, supported by blog posts, about risks in software testing. This 11-minute recording is followed by another one about How to prioritize risks for testing.

qase.io

AND...

Codex will review your code... 😂

Thanks for reading!

If you enjoy this newsletter and find it helpful in becoming a better tester, please consider sharing it with others.

Dawid Dylowicz

This RSS feed is published on https://softwaretestingweekly.com/. You can also subscribe via email.

】

评论

欢迎来到第309期！

正在寻找软件测试工具？

查看 TestVerse — 由 Anshita Bhasin 提供的包含 190 多种分类测试工具的免费目录。

如果您想了解有关如何使用它的更多信息，她编写了一份方便的指南。

测试愉快！ 🙂

大卫·迪洛维奇

赞助商链接

编码代理的发货速度加快了 55%。继续参加代理测试峰会。

外循环已成为人工智能生成代码的新瓶颈。与 mabl 和 QA 领导者一起参加为期半天的峰会，了解如何构建代理测试策略，与正在尝试新方法的同行建立联系，并在离开时更清楚地了解您的团队如何保持领先地位。 报名参加您附近的峰会。

mabl.com

新闻

人工智能正在改变软件测试。经过 20 年的 QA 工作，我可以告诉您这种转变是不同的。

Olga Manuilova 回顾了 20 年的 QA 经验，并解释了该行业如何从执行转向质量战略和风险管理。

此外，Simon Prior 分享了关于智能质量领导力：仍在形成的模型的一些新想法。

medium.com

质量不是测试问题

Alan Page 的一个很好的提醒，系统的质量生活，它应该是内置的，而不是经过测试的。

接下来是 Filip Hric 发表的一篇关于马斯洛之锤和 QA 的三个谎言的精彩帖子。

substack.com

重新思考 AI 支持的功能的测试策略

测试人工智能功能？ 伊尔凡·穆贾吉奇 分享一个测试策略，包括一个实用的框架，用于决定什么要自动化、什么要手动检查以及什么要监视。

您还可以按照Emily O'Connor和Richard Adams关于使用“5个为什么”的两种方法的建议方法：错误的根本原因并确定持续改进。

thegreenreport.blog

测试是系统思考

Patrick Prill 发表的一篇发人深省的文章，描述了如何使用 DSRP 心理模型（区别、系统、关系、视角）进行更好的测试。

此外，Patrick 还解释了为什么人工智能无法取代胶水工作。

wordpress.com

您爬上了测试金字塔。现在吃一块蛋奶冻片。

如果您根据每层提供的价值来考虑测试套件，而不仅仅是技术范围，结果会怎样？ Stuart Crocker 介绍了蛋奶冻切片作为测试金字塔的替代方案。

同时，Shubham Sharma 提出了测试格 - 一种评估测试成熟度的便捷方法。

dragonsforelevenses.com

自动化

AI 测试代理如何改变 Showpad 的质量保证

正在寻求有关如何将 AI 代理嵌入到日常 QA 工作中的建议？ Ganeshan Manickam 描述了他们如何为每个测试阶段构建小型代理以及他们从中学到了什么。

medium.com

混沌突变体：如果机器人每天晚上都试图破解您的代码怎么办？

Prateek Singh 概述了夜间突变测试设置，该设置随机挑选最近更改的文件、注入错误并显示您的测试会错过哪些文件。

此外，Thulane Khumalo 询问并解释：什么是 semtest？

medium.com

快速提供有价值的反馈

什么才是好的自动化测试？ Bas Dijkstra 将其分为四个角度并分享实用技巧，例如保持较小的测试范围和使用自定义断言消息。 Bas 还分享了我对测试自动化中“自我修复”的想法。

ontestautomation.com

为什么要分层测试框架人工智能时代架构仍然很重要

Josphine Job 详细介绍了测试自动化框架的六层，并解释了为什么人工智能没有取代它们，而是在顶部添加了一个编排层。

此外，Mohammad Faisal Khatri 展示了使用 Playwright 和 TypeScript 在 API 测试自动化中实现Builder Pattern + Faker 的示例。

medium.com

您编写了太多测试

如果您对集成测试没有信心，Chris Tonkinson 解释了为什么您应该减少集成测试，并使用场景树测试模式进行自上而下的覆盖。

类似地，Oliver Fries 描述了我如何停止将测试方法视为宗教，并开始将它们视为 TDD 和 BDD 背景下的工具。

medium.com

工具

使用 Playwright 页面对象模型 (POM) 创建弹性测试框架

如果您想构建一个可扩展的测试框架，Shawn Vernier 会在 Playwright 中展示页面对象模型设置和示例。

对此的一个很好的补充阅读是Aparna Mishra对Playwright POM（没有类）：使您的测试干净且可重用的看法。

ministryoftesting.com

Playprom：将 Playwright 测试运行转换为时间序列数据

想要查看一段时间内您的剧作家测试结果吗？ Lutfi Fitroh Hadi 构建了 Playprom - 一个将测试运行作为指标发送到 Prometheus 和 Grafana 的报告器。

同样，Vitali Haradkou为剧作家测试报告创建了一个方便的解决方案，在电子邮件中看起来确实不错。

medium.com

剧作家无障碍测试：斧头和灯塔错过了什么

如果您使用自动化工具进行辅助功能测试，您可能有兴趣向 David Mello 学习哪些扫描仪无法捕获的缺陷以及如何利用剧作家以获得更好的结果。

如果您使用 Cypress，Gleb Bahmutov 也可以帮助您：使用 Cypress 和 wick-a11y 测试 A11y插件。

davidmello.com

剧作家 MCP Vision + DevTools：同时查看页面和读取网络流量的 AI 代理

你知道剧作家MCP的能力模式吗？ Pramod Dutta 概述了快照、愿景和开发工具，并提供了何时使用每种工具以及如何组合它们的实际示例。

此外，Gurudatt SA 告诉我们为什么你的剧作家 getByRole 定位器不起作用 - 了解 ARIA 和可访问名称。

medium.com

UTML：用于浏览器自动化的 UI 测试标记语言

Kulanthaivelu Sankaran 提出了 UTML，这是一种用于浏览器测试的基于 JSON 的标记语言，您可以使用它来描述步骤，例如“单击登录按钮”，并让它处理其余的事情。您还可以阅读第 2 部分和第 3 部分。

medium.com

书籍

资深工程师之路解读

这是 Roy Straub 在重读主管工程师之路后的深思熟虑的反思，现在他已经成为技术主管并拥有不同的观点。

rstraub.com

视频

从开发人员到测试人员：引领人工智能驱动的测试世界的转变

David Burns 和 Benjamin Bischoff 就软件测试人员在人工智能驱动的世界中不断变化的角色进行了长达 46 分钟的富有洞察力的讨论。

同样，Alan Richardson 邀请 Krisztián Fischer 和 Toby Sears 谈论人工智能和软件测试。

youtube.com

如何识别对测试最重要的风险

Vitaly Sharovatov 开始了一系列关于软件测试风险的新录音，并得到博客文章的支持。这段 11 分钟的录音之后是另一段关于如何确定测试风险优先级的录音。

qase.io

并且...

Codex 将审核您的代码... 😂

感谢您的阅读！

如果您喜欢本新闻稿并发现它有助于成为更好的测试人员，请考虑与其他人分享。

大卫·迪洛维奇

<小时>

此 RSS 提要发布于 https://softwaretestingweekly.com/。 您还可以订阅 通过电子邮件。

item.title: 【Software Testing Weekly - Issue 308 - Apr 14th 2026】软件测试周刊 - 第 308 期 - 2026 年 4 月 14 日
item.description:
【

COMMENT

Welcome to the 308th issue!

How should we approach testing in the age of AI?

It's an increasingly important question we should be asking ourselves.

So today, I want to highlight this great, thought-provoking article by Carlos Arguelles, Senior Principal Engineer at Amazon:

The Outer Loop is Dead: Rethinking how we test.

Happy testing!

Dawid Dylowicz

Sponsored Link

Breakpoint 2026: Where QA Teams Figure Out AI Together

Most teams are navigating AI-driven testing without a playbook. Breakpoint 2026 is where QA leaders from NVIDIA, Mastercard, KPMG, Deloitte, and Microsoft share what's actually working — live sessions, real Q&A, and hands-on workshops. Join from wherever you are on 12–14 May 2026. Save your spot!

browserstack.com

NEWS

Drop the test column. Now what?

Ah, the famous "In Testing" column! There are pros and cons to it, and if you're thinking about removing it, Jitesh Gosai suggests which signals to put in place to keep work visible.

In relation to that, Shawn Vernier wrote a good article on Why software testers should write documentation even if no one else reads it.

substack.com

Should QA exist?

Controversial! Jade Rubick wrote on whether engineering orgs should have QA at all, sparking an intriguing discussion among the testing community.

On that note, someone also asked: With AI advancing so fast… is Developer or QA the safer career now?

rubick.com

Testing the "Yes-Man" in Your Pocket

This is a sobering read from Jeff Nyman on why AI is biased towards agreeing with you and why the evaluation should be broader than just user satisfaction scores.

That's why Huib Schoots says we should value Critical Thinking and Bram van den Reijen explains why Being right is not enough: testing, truth and responsibility.

testerstories.com

The Code Tsunami

Vernon Richards reflects on how being a tester is becoming less about providing information and more about designing the decision framework that enables others to release safely.

Similarly, Andrii Cheparskyi talks about The Hidden Cost of AI-Generated Test Code, while Gil Zilberfeld explains why The Knowledge Void Eats Quality for Breakfast.

substack.com

You're Not Meta: Build Fast Teams with Fast Tests

An interesting read from Sayo Oladeji who advises investing in faster local test execution, fewer flaky tests and a balanced test pyramid, based on lessons learned at Google and Meta.

Moreover, Sanjeev Kumar tells us How I measure quality without looking at bug counts.

medium.com

AUTOMATION

AI in Test Automation: Real Limitations vs. User Error

David Mello reviews the most common AI testing complaints and confirms or debugs them, noting whether they're caused by AI or people.

Moreover, Pramod Dutta explains Why AI Made QA Engineers Write MORE Code, Not Less.

davidmello.com

AI-Powered API Testing at Scale

Wondering how to develop and run API tests across several repositories? Nikhil Gupta shows how to achieve that with an AI-powered approach.

Similarly, Olamide Adebayo describes how We Replaced Hours of Manual API Testing With an AI Agent Running Integration Tests in Real Time.

medium.com

The Bottom-Up Secret to 100% Test Coverage at Enterprise Scale

Is 100% coverage even possible? Alexandre Mendes also doubted it because it feels impossible when you think top-down, but becomes easier when you compose upward from smaller pieces.

On top of that, Doğukan Aydoğdu shares insights into How We Build Bulletproof Integration Tests.

medium.com

Writing More Readable Tests

Solid post from Ürgo Ringo on emphasising what matters and what doesn't in test code. Especially handy now that LLMs benefit from readable tests too.

Speaking of that, someone asked the testing community: Performing Assertions within the Page Object itself — is it a good idea?

medium.com

TOOLS

Designing Realistic Load Test Scenarios with k6

Indra Aristya shows how to design k6 load tests that mimic actual user behaviour with gradual ramp-up, sustained peak, ramp-down and think time between actions. Much more meaningful than a flat 100 VUs.

What's more, Bob Chen describes in three parts why K6.js API Health Tests Are Not a Testing Problem.

medium.com

I Built a QA Quality Gate System With Claude Code Hooks. Every AI-Generated Test Now Passes My Standards Before It Exists.

Wondering how to stop Claude from writing tests with hardcoded credentials and CSS selectors? Pramod Dutta demonstrates how he set up four hooks as predictable quality gates.

medium.com

Playwright Fixture Scopes: Know When to Use What

I wonder if you're aware of this handy feature in Playwright fixtures. Gurudatt S A covers how and when to use the test, worker, and file scopes.

Moreover, Viatsheslav Pashanin advises on Eliminating Authentication Overhead in Playwright Automation.

medium.com

Testing LLM Outputs: A Hands-On Guide to DeepEval Metrics

Serhii Smetanskyi shares what each DeepEval metric actually does, what surprised him, and what he wishes someone had told him before starting. Great reference if you're testing LLM features.

Similarly, Katja Obring points out that The Hard Part of AI Evals Isn't the Tooling.

linkedin.com

WICK-DOM-OBSERVER: The Deterministic Cypress Plugin for Fast Spinners, Blinking Toasts, Optional Overlays

Tired of flaky Cypress tests caused by quickly changing web elements? Sebastian Clavijo Suero built a plugin that uses MutationObserver to catch those tricky UI elements in a deterministic way.

At the same time, Vitaly Skadorva describes how to do Accessible web testing with Cypress and Axe Core.

dev.to

BOOKS

Essential reading for people who are still responsible and serious in IT

Maaike Brinkhof shares five books that helped her think more critically about IT, AI hype, and productivity culture. From cognitive biases to the history of the Luddites, this is an interesting and somewhat unconventional reading list.

maaikebrinkhof.nl

Put quality at the centre of what you do — A Review of 'Out of the Crisis' by W. Edwards Deming

A short, useful review from Mike Harris of a classic quality management book Out of the Crisis that's still relevant decades later.

home.blog

VIDEOS

QA Job Market Reality Check

Once again, Alex Khvastovich is back with a regular overview of the QA job market, sharing insights and advice.

Additionally, Karthik KK shares a bit more about The AI Layoff Lie: What Big Tech Isn't Telling You.

youtube.com

AND...

Just an app update... 😂

Thanks for reading!

If you enjoy this newsletter and find it helpful in becoming a better tester, please consider sharing it with others.

Dawid Dylowicz

This RSS feed is published on https://softwaretestingweekly.com/. You can also subscribe via email.

】

评论

欢迎来到第308期！

人工智能时代我们应该如何进行测试？

这是我们应该问自己的一个越来越重要的问题。

今天，我想重点介绍亚马逊高级首席工程师 Carlos Arguelles 撰写的这篇伟大的、发人深省的文章：

外循环已死：重新思考我们的测试方式。

测试愉快！

大卫·迪洛维奇

赞助商链接

2026 年断点：QA 团队共同探索人工智能

大多数团队在没有剧本的情况下进行人工智能驱动的测试。 Breakpoint 2026 是来自 NVIDIA、Mastercard、KPMG、Deloitte 和 Microsoft 的 QA 领导者分享实际工作成果的平台 - 现场会议、真实问答和实践研讨会。 2026 年 5 月 12 日至 14 日，无论您身在何处，都可以参加。保留您的席位！

browserstack.com

新闻

删除测试列。现在怎么办？

啊，著名的“测试中”专栏！它有利有弊，如果您正在考虑删除它，Jitesh Gosai 建议放置哪些信号以保持工作可见。

与此相关，Shawn Vernier 写了一篇很好的文章，内容是为什么软件测试人员应该编写文档，即使没有其他人阅读它。

substack.com

应该存在质量检查吗？

有争议！ Jade Rubick 撰写了有关工程组织是否应该进行 QA 的文章，在测试社区中引发了一场有趣的讨论。

就此而言，有人还问：随着人工智能发展如此之快……现在开发人员还是 QA 是更安全的职业吗？

rubick.com

测试你口袋里的“唯唯诺诺”

这是 Jeff Nyman 的一篇发人深省的文章，解释了为什么人工智能偏向于同意你的观点，以及为什么评估应该更广泛，而不仅仅是用户满意度得分。

这就是为什么Huib Schoots说我们应该重视批判性思维和Bram van den Reijen 解释了为什么仅仅正确是不够的：测试、真相和责任。

testerstories.com

代码海啸

Vernon Richards 反思了作为一名测试人员如何不再仅仅提供信息，而更多地关注设计决策框架，使其他人能够安全地发布。

同样，Andrii Cheparskyi 谈论了人工智能生成的测试代码的隐性成本，而 Gil Zilberfeld 解释了为什么知识空白吃早餐的质量。

substack.com

你不是元：通过快速测试构建快速团队

Sayo Oladeji 的有趣读物，他根据 Google 和 Meta 的经验教训，建议投资于更快的本地测试执行、更少的不稳定测试和平衡的测试金字塔。

此外，Sanjeev Kumar 告诉我们如何在不查看错误数量的情况下衡量质量。

medium.com

自动化

测试自动化中的人工智能：实际限制与用户错误

David Mello 回顾了最常见的 AI 测试投诉并确认或调试它们，并指出它们是由 AI 还是人造成的。

此外，Pramod Dutta 解释了为什么 AI 让 QA 工程师编写更多代码，而不是更少。

davidmello.com

大规模人工智能驱动的 API 测试

想知道如何跨多个存储库开发和运行 API 测试？ Nikhil Gupta 展示了如何通过人工智能驱动的方法实现这一目标。

类似地，Olamide Adebayo 描述了我们如何用实时运行集成测试的 AI 代理取代了数小时的手动 API 测试。

medium.com

企业规模 100% 测试覆盖率的自下而上秘诀

100% 的覆盖率是否可能？ Alexandre Mendes 也对此表示怀疑，因为感觉不可能n 你是自上而下思考的，但当你从小块向上组合时会变得更容易。

除此之外，Doğukan Aydoğdu 分享了对我们如何构建防弹集成测试的见解。

medium.com

编写更具可读性的测试

来自 Ürgo Ringo 的可靠帖子，强调了测试代码中重要的内容和不重要的内容。现在法学硕士也受益于可读的测试，这尤其方便。

说到这里，有人问测试社区：在页面对象本身内执行断言 - 这是一个好主意吗？

medium.com

工具

使用 k6 设计真实的负载测试场景

Indra Aristya 展示了如何设计 k6 负载测试，通过逐渐上升、持续峰值、下降和操作之间的思考时间来模拟实际用户行为。比固定的 100 个 VU 更有意义。

此外，Bob Chen分三个部分描述了为什么K6.js API 健康测试不是测试问题。

medium.com

我使用 Claude Code Hooks 构建了 QA 质量门系统。现在，每个人工智能生成的测试在出现之前就已经通过了我的标准。

想知道如何阻止 Claude 使用硬编码凭据和 CSS 选择器编写测试？ Pramod Dutta 演示了他如何设置四个钩子作为可预测的质量门。

medium.com

剧作家固定范围：知道何时使用什么

我想知道您是否知道Playwright装置中的这个方便的功能。 Gurudatt S A 介绍了如何以及何时使用测试、工作人员和文件范围。

此外，Viatsheslav Pashanin 建议消除剧作家自动化中的身份验证开销。

medium.com

测试 LLM 输出：DeepEval 指标实践指南

Serhii Smetanskyi 分享了每个 DeepEval 指标的实际用途，w这让他感到惊讶，他希望有人在开始之前告诉他这件事。如果您正在测试 LLM 功能，这是很好的参考。

同样，Katja Obring 指出，AI 评估的困难部分不是工具。

linkedin.com

WICK-DOM-OBSERVER：用于快速旋转、闪烁 Toast、可选叠加的确定性 Cypress 插件

厌倦了因快速变化的网络元素而导致的片状 Cypress 测试？ Sebastian Clavijo Suero 构建了一个插件，它使用 MutationObserver 以确定性的方式捕获那些棘手的 UI 元素。

同时，Vitaly Skadorva 介绍了如何使用 Cypress 和 Ax Core 进行可访问的 Web 测试。

dev.to

书籍

对于仍然对 IT 行业有责任感和认真态度的人来说，这是一本必备读物

Maaike Brinkhof 分享了五本书，帮助她更加批判性地思考 IT、AI 炒作和生产力文化。从认知偏见到勒德分子的历史，这是一个有趣且有些非传统的阅读清单。

maaikebrinkhof.nl

将质量置于工作的中心 — W. Edwards Deming 对《走出危机》的评论

Mike Harris 对一本经典质量管理书籍走出危机进行了简短而有用的评论，几十年后仍然具有现实意义。

home.blog

视频

QA 就业市场现实检查

Alex Khvastovich 再次回归，定期概述 QA 就业市场，分享见解和建议。

此外，Karthik KK 分享了更多关于人工智能裁员谎言：大型科技公司没有告诉你的事情。

youtube.com

并且...

只是一个应用程序更新... 😂

感谢您的阅读！

如果您喜欢本新闻稿并发现它有助于成为更好的测试人员，请考虑与其他人分享。

<一小时f="https://cur.at/p7f9xfT?m=rss">大卫·迪洛维奇

<小时>

此 RSS 提要发布于 https://softwaretestingweekly.com/。 您还可以订阅 通过电子邮件。

item.title: 【Software Testing Weekly - Issue 307 - Apr 1st 2026】软件测试周刊 - 第 307 期 - 2026 年 4 月 1 日
item.description:
【

COMMENT

Welcome to the 307th issue!

AI is the hottest topic right now. And for a reason!

So today, I'm bringing to you two big discussions among the testing community:

  • Do you use any tools or AI for testing?
  • AI use cases for QA

As a bonus, I also recommend Chris Kenst's take on My AI Tooling Stack: The March 2026 Edition.

Happy testing!

Dawid Dylowicz

NEWS

AI writes the code. But who tests it?

Good question. Axel Kirchner makes the case that AI-assisted development doesn't reduce the need for quality assurance but changes where that work needs to happen.

Similarly, Irfan Mujagić also asks: Who Tests the Tests? AI, QA, and the Verification Paradox.

medium.com

Architecting for Scale: Why "Writing Tests" Is No Longer the Point

Karthik Sivakumar argues that the biggest drag on engineering velocity isn't bad code — it's poorly designed test infrastructure.

What's more, Betty Lin shares about What AI Still Cannot See in Software Quality (Part 1): Requirement Conflicts, followed by parts two, three and four.

medium.com

From SDET to AI-augmented Quality Engineer: Where AI Actually Belongs

Not all parts of test automation benefit from AI in the same way. Viktar Karanevich points to a few areas and shares an SDET transformation roadmap.

aiqualityengineer.cc

QA engineers who will be the most successful over the next five years

Wondering how to keep up with the changing world of software? Ben Fellows sees the tester role changing too and suggests what skills will be the most important.

Additionally, Neil Duggan points out why Your QA Team in 2028 Doesn't Look Like Your QA Team Today.

linkedin.com

Quality Engineering with AI

This is good advice from Callum Akehurst-Ryan on how QA teams should adapt their approach when developers are leaning heavily on AI assistance.

And there's a reason why Dan Belcher announces we live in The Most Exciting Moment in Software History due to the recent advancement of LLM models.

cakehurstryan.com

What I Actually Look For When I Interview QA Engineers (And How You Can Prepare)

Preparing for your next QA role? Bartosz Kustra breaks down what actually matters to interviewers in the current market.

Similarly, Joshua Bihun also advises on Hiring QA Engineers: Start With What You Actually Need.

medium.com

AUTOMATION

Evolving POM: From Page Objects to Agent-Friendly Design

This is a good walkthrough from Martín Marchetto on how to structure test code so that both humans and AI agents can work with it.

medium.com

From a long list to a clear signal: baseline-driven accessibility reporting

Accessibility test reports can be noisy, but Benjamin Bischoff and the Trivago team found a better, more actionable way of dealing with it.

trivago.com

Manual QA + AI = Automation Tester

Pramod Dutta ran an interesting experiment, observing manual testers implement test suites much faster without a traditional learning path, thanks to AI.

Also, Megan Ozanne wrote a good article about Coaching with code: using AI to learn how to automate tests.

medium.com

Stop Writing API Tests Manually — Let Your OpenAPI Spec Do the Work

David Mello presents a tool that generates API test cases directly from an existing contract and proves to be surprisingly good at covering edge cases.

davidmello.com

What If AI Could Tell QA What Your Pull Request Might Break?

An interesting idea from Mehmet Serhat Özdursun, suggesting using AI to analyse git diffs and give testers a structured view of where a change is most likely to cause problems.

devgenius.io

Sponsored Link

If there's one workshop this year that will genuinely change how you approach AI testing, this is it.

Join Ivan Davidov and Debbie O'Brien on April 29 for a hands-on deep dive into building Playwright architectures where AI actually works reliably at scale. This is the session your testing workflow has been missing.

Limited seats, use code PLAYWRIGHT40 for 40% off.

👉 Register now before it sells out.

eventbrite.com

TOOLS

3 Design Principles for Creating Agent Skills

Skills have become the default way to provide context and instructions to AI agents. But how to do it right? Angie Jones shares some tips.

Similarly, Gurudatt S A advises to Stop Re-Explaining Your Test Conventions to Claude — Use SKILL.md and Deep Shah explains why I Stopped Writing Prompts Every Day.

angiejones.tech

GitHub Copilot in QA — What Nobody Tells You Until You're Already Using It

Swati Sabharwal spent six months figuring out how to make AI coding assistance work in a real team environment and shares her insights.

Speaking of that, James Kip explains why Your Linter Can't Save You: Using .github/copilot-instructions.md to Catch What Static Analysis Misses.

medium.com

Maestro at Scale: Architecting a Deterministic Mobile Test Platform

Emre Erkek describes in detail how they implemented an end-to-end mobile automation with Maestro across multiple business domains.

medium.com

Migrating from Selenium to Playwright: The Complete Guide

Thinking about switching to Playwright? Asjad Khan wrote an extensive overview on how to do it right, especially about the challenging parts.

currents.dev

VIDEOS

I Built Karpathy's AutoResearch for Playwright Testing

In this 10-minute overview, Karthik KK demonstrates a custom-built autonomous UI testing agent with Playwright, inspired by autoresearch's approach.

youtube.com

Playwright CLI Tutorial

Playwright CLI was released just a few weeks ago and it's now the default interface for AI agents. If you want to see it in action, Lucas Smit recorded a helpful 18-minute demo.

youtube.com

AND...

Bugs in 2026... 😅

Thanks for reading!

If you enjoy this newsletter and find it helpful in becoming a better tester, please consider sharing it with others.

Dawid Dylowicz

This RSS feed is published on https://softwaretestingweekly.com/. You can also subscribe via email.

】

评论

欢迎来到第307期！

人工智能是当前最热门的话题。这是有原因的！

所以今天，我将为您带来测试社区中的两个重大讨论：

  • 您是否使用任何工具或人工智能进行测试？
  • 用于质量检查的人工智能用例

作为奖励，我还推荐 Chris Kenst 对我的 AI 工具堆栈：2026 年 3 月版的看法。

测试愉快！

大卫·迪洛维奇

新闻

AI 编写代码。但谁来测试它呢？

好问题。 Axel Kirchner 证明人工智能辅助开发并没有减少质量保证的需求，而是改变了需要进行工作的地方。

同样，Irfan Mujagić 也问：谁来测试测试？人工智能、质量保证和验证悖论。

medium.com

规模化架构：为什么“编写测试”不再是重点

Karthik Sivakumar 认为，工程速度的最大拖累并不是糟糕的代码，而是设计糟糕的测试基础设施。

此外，Betty Lin 分享了人工智能在软件质量中仍然看不到的东西（第 1 部分）：需求冲突，随后是两部分， 三个和四个。

medium.com

从 SDET 到 AI 增强质量工程师：AI 的真正归属

并非测试自动化的所有部分都以同样的方式受益于人工智能。 Viktar Karanevich 指出了几个领域并分享了 SDET 转型路线图。

aiqualityengineer.cc

未来五年内最成功的 QA 工程师

想知道如何跟上不断变化的软件世界？ Ben Fellows 发现测试人员的角色也在发生变化，并建议哪些技能是最重要的。

此外，Neil Duggan 指出了原因2028 年的 QA 团队与今天的 QA 团队不同。

linkedin.com

人工智能质量工程

这是 Callum Akehurst-Ryan 提出的关于当开发人员严重依赖人工智能辅助时 QA 团队应如何调整其方法的好建议。

Dan Belcher 宣布我们生活在软件历史上最激动人心的时刻，这是有原因的，因为 LLM 模型的最新进展。

cakehurstryan.com

面试 QA 工程师时我实际上在寻找什么（以及如何准备）

正在为您的下一个 QA 角色做准备吗？ Bartosz Kustra 详细分析了当前市场中对面试官来说真正重要的因素。

同样，Joshua Bihun 也就雇用 QA 工程师：从您实际需要的开始提出建议。

medium.com

自动化

不断发展的 POM：从页面对象到代理友好的设计

这是 Martín Marchetto 的一个很好的演练，介绍了如何构建测试代码，以便人类和 AI 代理都可以使用它。

medium.com

从长长的列表到清晰的信号：基线驱动的无障碍报告

辅助功能测试报告可能会很混乱，但 Benjamin Bischoff 和 Trivago 团队找到了一种更好、更可行的处理方法。

trivago.com

手动 QA + AI = 自动化测试仪

Pramod Dutta 进行了一项有趣的实验，观察手动测试人员在没有传统学习路径的情况下更快地实现测试套件，这要归功于人工智能。

此外，Megan Ozanne 写了一篇关于指导代码：使用 AI 学习如何自动化测试的好文章。

medium.com

停止手动编写 API 测试 - 让您的 OpenAPI 规范来完成工作

大卫·梅洛 提出了一种直接从现有合约生成 API 测试用例的工具，并且事实证明在覆盖边缘情况方面出人意料地出色。

davidmello.com

如果人工智能可以告诉 QA 你的 Pull 请求可能会破坏什么？

来自 Mehmet Serhat Özdursun 的一个有趣想法，建议使用 AI 来分析 git diff，并为测试人员提供结构化视图，了解更改最有可能导致问题的位置。

devgenius.io

赞助商链接

如果说今年有一个研讨会能够真正改变您进行 AI 测试的方式，那就是它了。

4 月 29 日与 Ivan Davidov 和 Debbie O'Brien 一起深入实践，深入构建 Playwright 架构，让 AI 能够在规模上真正可靠地运行。这是您的测试工作流程缺少的会话。

席位有限，使用代码PLAYWRIGHT40可享受 40% 的折扣。

👉在售完之前立即注册。

eventbrite.com

工具

创建代理技能的 3 条设计原则

技能已成为向人工智能代理提供上下文和指令的默认方式。但怎样做才是正确的呢？ Angie Jones 分享了一些技巧。

同样，Gurudatt SA 建议停止向 Claude 重新解释您的测试约定 - 使用 SKILL.md 和深入Shah 解释了为什么我停止每天写提示。

angiejones.tech

QA 中的 GitHub Copilot — 在您使用之前没有人告诉您的内容

Swati Sabharwal 花了六个月的时间研究如何让人工智能编码辅助在真实的团队环境中发挥作用，并分享了她的见解。

说到这里，James Kip 解释了为什么你的 Linter 无法拯救你：使用 .github/copilot-instructions.md 来捕获静态分析遗漏的内容。

medium.com

大规模大师：构建确定性移动测试平台

Emre Erkek 详细描述了他们如何实现端到端- 跨多个业务领域使用 Maestro 实现移动自动化。

medium.com

从 Selenium 迁移到 Playwright：完整指南

考虑切换到剧作家？ Asjad Khan 撰写了关于如何正确执行此操作的详细概述，尤其是关于具有挑战性的部分。

currents.dev

视频

我为剧作家测试构建了 Karpathy 的 AutoResearch

在这个 10 分钟的概述中，Karthik KK 演示了一个使用 Playwright 定制的自主 UI 测试代理，其灵感来自于 autoresearch方法。

youtube.com

Playwright CLI 教程

Playwright CLI 于几周前发布，现在它是 AI 代理的默认界面。如果您想查看实际效果，Lucas Smit 录制了一个有用的 18 分钟演示。

youtube.com

并且...

2026 年的错误... 😅

感谢您的阅读！

如果您喜欢本新闻稿并发现它有助于成为更好的测试人员，请考虑与其他人分享。

大卫·迪洛维奇

<小时>

此 RSS 提要发布于 https://softwaretestingweekly.com/。 您还可以订阅 通过电子邮件。

item.title: 【Software Testing Weekly - Issue 306 - Mar 24th 2026】软件测试周刊 - 第 306 期 - 2026 年 3 月 24 日
item.description:
【

COMMENT

Welcome to the 306th issue!

Money — this topic comes back regularly among the testing community.

So today, I want to highlight this: What is your current salary as a Quality Engineer?

This megathread with over 200 replies should give you some insights.

Happy testing! 🙂

PS. You can also check out previous posts and surveys on QA salaries.

Dawid Dylowicz

Sponsored Link

The Quality Leadership Academy: Take your leadership skills to the next level

Most QA executives are trained to execute tests. Few are trained to lead a software testing strategy. The Quality Leadership Academy (QLA), founded by PractiTest, is a selective professional development program designed to bridge the gap between technical testing and strategic leadership. Apply now.

practitest.com

NEWS

More Output Is Not More Value, or Why AI Might Break Your System

AI helps us move faster, but as Patrick Prill neatly points out, it also generates risks faster. He also advocates that You Ship It, You Own It.

Similarly, Paul Seaman wrote a decent article about The illusion (and confusion) of speed.

wordpress.com

Stop Testing. Start Engineering Quality.

Martin Ivanov explains why relying solely on testers for verification is not the best option and describes different stages of maturity towards building quality directly into development.

Similarly, Joshua Bihun outlines the journey From QA to Quality Engineering: Modernizing Your Approach.

medium.com

The Autonomous Testing Tipping Point (Part 1)

"Autonomous Testing isn't about removing humans. It's all about removing the friction that stops humans from doing their best work.

Dan Ashby shares some good thoughts on AI-powered testing. You can also read the second part.

Moreover, Sam White shares insights into How I failed to convince a room of developers, that AI can harm software quality…

danashby.co.uk

Urgency Is Killing Your QA: Here's How to Save It (Before It's Too Late)

There are some solid tips from Marina Jordão on how to meet deadlines without compromising on testing, thanks to proper prioritisation, collaboration and quick exploratory checks.

Also, Matthew Sullivan says: Software QA Didn't Die — It Never Started.

medium.com

Why would I even want to generate test cases with AI?

An interesting article by Maaret Pyhäjärvi explaining why testing with AI should be focused on scaling learning and results, not predefined steps.

On the other hand, Peter Houghton explores the idea of using Validators as coding agent specifications.

visible-quality.blogspot.com

AUTOMATION

AI-Assisted Development is the New Test Automation

That's an insightful take from Stuart Thomas on how AI mirrors early automation days with faster delivery and changing roles toward higher-level design and risk management.

On that note, Andrii Cheparskyi investigates AI in Testing #10: Spec-Driven Development — BDD's Second Chance or Just More Docs?

thequalityduck.co.uk

Goal-Based Agentic Testing for Mobile Applications

Russell Morley proposes an interesting concept — instead of providing detailed test scripts, he suggests setting a goal for an AI agent and letting it try to reach it within the app.

And here's a relevant Reddit discussion about the state of Mobile automation as of 2026.

russmorley.net

How to Measure E2E Test Coverage Without a Standard Tool

How do you track what your E2E tests actually cover? Josphine Job describes a simple way to map tests to screens or flows instead of lines of code.

medium.com

Stop Copying Prod Into Dev: Test Data Strategies That Actually Scale

Want to have prod-like data for testing? Joel Dickson shares his point of view on why copying production data might not be the best option and suggests better ways, such as seeding, builders and isolated databases.

medium.com

Writing tests with Claude Code — Part 1: Initial results

Claude Code seems to be getting a lot of attention these days, but is it that effective? Bas Dijkstra used it for generating API tests and verified the results with mutated tests.

Similarly, Parinder Singh Raina put together a complete demo of Building an E2E test coverage with Claude Code, while Deep Shah advises on The SDET's Secret Weapon in 2026: Prompt Engineering.

ontestautomation.com

Sponsored Link

Lynqa: Execute your tests with AI

Lynqa executes your manual test cases as-is from Xray and provides step-by-step, traceable results automatically synced back to Xray. It helps handle regression peaks, saves time and execution costs, and lets QA focus on higher-value testing. Try it with 10 free credits.

smartesting.com

TOOLS

Making Sense of Playwright MCP, CLI, and Skills

Playwright provides several AI-friendly solutions. Gurudatt S A compares them and advises what to use.

Similarly, someone on Reddit is looking for advice on how to do Playwright Test Automation with AI effectively.

medium.com

pytest — The Python Testing Framework That Actually Makes You Want to Write Tests

Writing test in Python? You might be interested in Kapil Dagur's detailed overview of the recently released pytest 9.0 version and the benefits it brings.

medium.com

Why We Chose Playwright Over Cypress

Wondering whether to choose Playwright or Cypress for test automation? John Gluck tested both and wrote a great, detailed comparison.

Also, Yevhen Laichenkov concisely describes how to use Playwright step decorator for better test reporting.

qawolf.com

Your K6 Tests Are Lying to You (And It's Not K6's Fault)

Using k6 for load testing? J. Alexander explains why it's crucial to understand how Virtual Users work and why iteration time and sleeps can change throughput more than you may expect.

medium.com

AND...

Some projects be like... 😂

Thanks for reading!

If you enjoy this newsletter and find it helpful in becoming a better tester, please consider sharing it with others.

Dawid Dylowicz

This RSS feed is published on https://softwaretestingweekly.com/. You can also subscribe via email.

】

评论

欢迎来到第306期！

金钱——这个话题经常在测试社区中出现。

所以今天，我想强调这一点：您作为质量工程师目前的薪水是多少？

这个拥有超过 200 个回复的超级线程应该可以为您提供一些见解。

测试愉快！ 🙂

PS。您还可以查看之前关于 QA 薪资的帖子和调查。

大卫·迪洛维奇

赞助商链接

质量领导力学院：将您的领导技能提升到新的水平

大多数 QA 主管都接受过执行测试的培训。很少有人接受过领导软件测试策略的培训。 质量领导力学院 (QLA) 由 PractiTest 创立，是一个选择性专业发展计划，旨在弥合技术测试和战略领导力之间的差距。 立即申请。

practitest.com

新闻

更多输出并不意味着更多价值，或者为什么人工智能可能会破坏您的系统

人工智能帮助我们更快地行动，但正如 Patrick Prill 巧妙指出的那样，它也更快地产生风险。他还主张你发货，你就拥有它。

同样，Paul Seaman 写了一篇关于速度的幻觉（和混乱）的不错的文章。

wordpress.com

停止测试。启动工程质量。

Martin Ivanov 解释了为什么仅依靠测试人员进行验证并不是最佳选择，并描述了直接将质量构建到开发中的不同成熟阶段。

同样，Joshua Bihun 概述了从 QA 到质量工程：现代化您的方法的历程。

medium.com

自主测试临界点（第 1 部分）

<块引用>

“自主测试并不是要消除人类。而是要消除阻碍人类发挥最佳工作效率的摩擦。

Dan Ashby 分享了一些关于人工智能驱动测试的好想法。您还可以阅读第二部分。

此外，Sam White 分享了关于我如何未能说服一屋子开发人员相信人工智能会损害软件质量的见解......

danashby.co.uk

紧迫性正在扼杀您的质量检查：以下是如何挽救它（在为时已晚之前）

Marina Jordão 提供了一些可靠的建议，说明如何通过适当的优先级排序、协作和快速探索性检查，在不影响测试的情况下按时完成任务。

此外，Matthew Sullivan 表示：软件 QA 并未消亡 — 它从未开始。

medium.com

为什么我想用 AI 生成测试用例？

Maaret Pyhäjärvi 撰写的一篇有趣的文章解释了为什么人工智能测试应侧重于扩展学习和结果，而不是预定义的步骤。

另一方面，Peter Houghton 探索了使用验证器作为编码代理规范的想法。

visible-quality.blogspot.com

自动化

人工智能辅助开发是新的测试自动化

这是 Stuart Thomas 的富有洞察力的观点，内容涉及人工智能如何通过更快的交付以及将角色转变为更高级别的设计和风险管理来反映早期自动化时代。

就此而言，Andrii Cheparskyi 调查了测试中的人工智能#10：规范驱动开发 — BDD 的第二次机会还是更多文档？

thequalityduck.co.uk

移动应用程序基于目标的代理测试

Russell Morley 提出了一个有趣的概念 - 他建议为 AI 代理设定一个目标，并让它尝试在应用程序内实现目标，而不是提供详细的测试脚本。

这里有一个关于 截至 2026 年移动自动化状态的相关 Reddit 讨论。

russmorley.net

如何在不使用标准工具的情况下衡量 E2E 测试覆盖率

您如何跟踪 E2E 测试的实际覆盖范围？ Josphine Job 描述了一种将测试映射到屏幕或流程而不是代码行的简单方法。

medium.com

停止将产品复制到开发中：测试实际可扩展的数据策略

想要获得类似产品的数据进行测试吗？ Joel Dickson 分享了他关于为什么复制生产数据可能不是最佳选择的观点，并提出了更好的方法，例如播种、构建器和隔离数据库。

medium.com

使用 Claude 代码编写测试 - 第 1 部分：初步结果

Claude Code 最近似乎受到了很多关注，但它有那么有效吗？ Bas Dijkstra 使用它来生成 API 测试，并通过变异测试验证结果。

同样，Parinder Singh Raina 整理了使用 Claude Code 构建 E2E 测试覆盖范围的完整演示，而 Deep Shah 提供了建议SDET 2026 年的秘密武器：快速工程。

ontestautomation.com

赞助商链接

Lynqa：使用 AI 执行测试

Lynqa 按原样从 Xray 执行手动测试用例，并提供自动同步回 Xray 的分步、可追踪结果。它有助于处理回归峰值，节省时间和执行成本，并让 QA 专注于更高价值的测试。 使用 10 个免费积分尝试。

smarttesting.com

工具

了解剧作家 MCP、CLI 和技能

Playwright 提供了多种人工智能友好的解决方案。 Gurudatt S A 对它们进行了比较并提出了使用建议。

同样，Reddit 上有人正在寻求有关如何有效地利用 AI 进行剧作家测试自动化的建议。

medium.com

pytest — 真正让您想要编写测试的 Python 测试框架

用Python编写测试？您可能对 Kapil Dagur 对最近发布的 pytest 9.0 版本及其带来的好处的详细概述。

medium.com

为什么我们选择剧作家而不是赛普拉斯

想知道是选择 Playwright 还是Cypress 进行测试自动化？ John Gluck 对两者进行了测试，并写了一篇精彩而详细的比较。

此外，Yevhen Laichenkov 简要介绍了如何使用Playwright 步骤装饰器来获得更好的测试报告。

qawolf.com

您的 K6 测试在骗您（这不是 K6 的错）

使用 k6 进行负载测试？ J. Alexander 解释了为什么了解虚拟用户的工作原理至关重要，以及为什么迭代时间和睡眠对吞吐量的影响超出了您的预期。

medium.com

并且...

有些项目就像... 😂

感谢您的阅读！

如果您喜欢本新闻稿并发现它有助于成为更好的测试人员，请考虑与其他人分享。

大卫·迪洛维奇

<小时>

此 RSS 提要发布于 https://softwaretestingweekly.com/。 您还可以订阅 通过电子邮件。

item.title: 【Software Testing Weekly - Issue 305 - Mar 11th 2026】软件测试周刊 - 第 305 期 - 2026 年 3 月 11 日
item.description:
【

COMMENT

Welcome to the 305th issue!

Skills are the new way for instructing AI agents to generate better results.

And now we have it for testing, too!

  • QASkills.sh by Pramod Dutta
  • Playwright Skills by Walter Galvāo
  • Playwright Skill Guides by Pratik Patel

Happy (skills) testing! 🙂

Dawid Dylowicz

NEWS

The Death of Determinism: How AI Forces Us to Rethink Testing

Padget Avery explains how testing AI differs from the traditional approach, requiring moving from strict assertions to thresholds, metrics, and human review in pipelines.

Moreover, Katja Obring shares thoughts on What I don't understand about AI evals (yet) and how The AI evals field chose a flawed tool and stuck with it.

medium.com

We Need to Stop Queueing Quality

Should teams keep a separate testing stage on their boards? Jitesh Gosai shares a simple analogy to show how it can delay feedback.

Similarly, Joshua Bihun wrote good articles on What Acceptance Criteria Actually Ought to Say and how to Shift-Left Quality From Day One.

substack.com

When building is cheap, quality becomes a bigger differentiator for your software product

AI speeds up coding, but at what cost? Esben Bager raises a good point — software advantage will now be driven primarily by quality and reliability rather than the number of features.

Similarly, Josh Grant points out that Software Quality is Heading Toward a Revolution and, in a similar context, Maaike Brinkhof says to Stop selling gold when all you've got to offer is a process.

medium.com

Why I am not worried about AI replacing me

With the rapid, constant development of AI, you may wonder whether it poses a threat to the tester's role. Matthew Sullivan doesn't think so, for a few reasons.

Similarly, Brad DerManouelian explains why AI Isn't Killing Manual QA. It's Supercharging It.

medium.com

AUTOMATION

AI and Testing: Using Local Models for Testing

Jeff Nyman continues a detailed series of articles on AI in testing, and shows how to set up and run a model on your machine to create automated tests with evaluation metrics. You can also read a follow-up article about Using Model Pipelines for Testing.

testerstories.com

My Engineering Manager asked for a test coverage dashboard. Here's what I built

Who wasn't in that situation? It's interesting to see how Osanda Deshan Nimalarathna approached building a dashboard for tracking automated test coverage.

medium.com

Only 41% of Testing Is Automated in 2026. More AI Tools Won't Fix That.

Pramod Dutta shares insights from the recent Leapwork survey on the state of test automation, revealing some (un)surprising data.

medium.com

Rethinking performance testing for the modern web

An insightful article from Shyamal Raju explaining why browser performance is important for user experience and demonstrating how to include browser flows in load testing.

On this note, Oleh Koren shares good thoughts on Why Percentiles Matter More Than Average Response Time in Performance Testing.

medium.com

Test Plan Driven Development (TPDD): A Developer-Friendly Alternative to TDD

This sounds novel. Vigor Zang recognises the friction with practising Test-Driven Development and suggests a different approach to ensure quality since the early stages of the software development life cycle.

Moreover, Sefa Esendemir wrote about more levels of the test pyramid — From Writing Code to Breaking It.

medium.com

TOOLS

17 Playwright Testing Mistakes You Should Avoid

There is some great, practical advice on how to improve your Playwright tests to run more smoothly and fail less often. By Yevhen Laichenkov.

elaichenkov.github.io

Deep Dive into Playwright CLI: Token Efficient Browser Automation

Two weeks ago, I mentioned the release of Playwright CLI. Now we can learn more about it, thanks to this detailed overview by Pratik Patel.

testdino.com

k6-cucumber-steps: Run K6 Performance Tests with Cucumber BDD Syntax

Cucumber for performance tests? Chetachi Enyimiri thought "Why not!" and demonstrated how to achieve that with k6.

Also, Marlo Henrique advises Using SharedArray with k6.

medium.com

Making Code Coverage Work with Playwright

Sayed Afnan Khazi tells us how they've been looking for a way to get Playwright test coverage in a non-trivial project setup and how the playwright-coverage library made it happen.

Speaking of that, Bhavik Gandhi wrote a helpful guide on How to Intercept and Modify Network Responses with Playwright.

medium.com

Migrating From Cypress.env To cy.env and Cypress.expose Methods

There's a major change coming in Cypress v16 and Gleb Bahmutov shows how to prepare for handling the environment variables and secrets in a new way.

Also, Jennifer Shehane has just announced another improvement that allows you to Fix Cypress CI failures caused by no spec files found.

glebbahmutov.com

AND...

"Quality is too high..." 🤣

Thanks for reading!

If you enjoy this newsletter and find it helpful in becoming a better tester, please consider sharing it with others.

Dawid Dylowicz

This RSS feed is published on https://softwaretestingweekly.com/. You can also subscribe via email.

】

评论

欢迎来到第305期！

技能是指导人工智能代理产生更好结果的新方法。

现在我们也可以进行测试了！

  • QASkills.sh 作者：Pramod Dutta
  • 剧作家技巧作者：Walter Galvāo
  • 剧作家技能指南，作者：Pratik Patel

祝（技能）测试愉快！ 🙂

大卫·迪洛维奇

新闻

决定论的消亡：人工智能如何迫使我们重新思考测试

Padget Avery 解释了测试 AI 与传统方法的不同之处，需要从严格的断言转向管道中的阈值、指标和人工审核。

此外，Katja Obring 分享了关于我对 AI 评估尚不了解的部分以及AI 评估领域如何选择了有缺陷的工具并陷入困境的想法有了它。

medium.com

我们需要停止排队质量

团队应该在他们的板上保留一个单独的测试阶段吗？ Jitesh Gosai 分享了一个简单的类比来展示它如何延迟反馈。

同样，Joshua Bihun 撰写了关于验收标准实际上应该说的内容以及如何从当天开始质量左移的好文章一个。

substack.com

当构建成本低廉时，质量就成为软件产品的更大差异化因素

人工智能加快了编码速度，但代价是什么？ Esben Bager 提出了一个很好的观点——软件优势现在主要由质量和可靠性驱动，而不是由功能数量驱动。

同样，Josh Grant 指出软件质量正在走向革命，并且在类似的背景下，Maaike Brinkhof 对当您提供的只是一个过程时，请停止出售黄金。

medium.com

为什么我不担心人工智能取代我

随着人工智能的快速、不断发展，您可能想知道它是否对测试人员的角色构成威胁。 Matthew Sullivan 出于一些原因并不这么认为。

同样，Brad DerManouelian 解释了为什么人工智能不会杀死手动 QA。它正在增压。

medium.com

自动化

人工智能和测试：使用本地模型进行测试

Jeff Nyman 继续撰写有关测试中的 AI 的详细系列文章，并展示如何在计算机上设置和运行模型，以使用评估指标创建自动化测试。您还可以阅读有关使用模型管道进行测试的后续文章。

testerstories.com

我的工程经理要求提供测试覆盖率仪表板。这是我构建的

谁没有遇到过这种情况？了解 Osanda Deshan Nimalarathna 如何构建用于跟踪自动化测试覆盖率的仪表板是很有趣的。

medium.com

2026 年只有 41% 的测试实现自动化。更多的 AI 工具无法解决这个问题。

Pramod Dutta 分享了最近 Leapwork 调查中关于测试自动化状况的见解，揭示了一些（不）令人惊讶的数据。

medium.com

重新思考现代网络的性能测试

来自 Shyamal Raju 的一篇富有洞察力的文章，解释了为什么浏览器性能对于用户体验很重要，并演示了如何在负载测试中包含浏览器流。

在此说明中，Oleh Koren 分享了关于为什么在性能测试中百分位数比平均响应时间更重要的好想法。

medium.com

测试计划驱动开发 (TPDD)：开发者友好的 TDD 替代方案

这听起来很新奇。 Vigor Zang 认识到实践测试驱动开发的摩擦，并提出了一种不同的方法从软件开发生命周期的早期阶段开始，就采取了不同的方法来确保质量。

此外，Sefa Esendemir 撰写了有关测试金字塔的更多级别的文章 - 从编写代码到破坏代码。

medium.com

工具

您应该避免的 17 个剧作家测试错误

关于如何改进 Playwright 测试以更顺利地运行并更少失败，有一些很棒的实用建议。作者：叶夫亨·莱琴科夫。

elaichenkov.github.io

深入了解 Playwright CLI：令牌高效的浏览器自动化

两周前，我提到Playwright CLI的发布。现在，得益于 Pratik Patel 的详细概述，我们可以了解更多信息。

testdino.com

k6-cucumber-steps：使用 Cucumber BDD 语法运行 K6 性能测试

黄瓜用于性能测试？ Chetachi Enyimiri 心想“为什么不呢！”并演示了如何使用 k6 实现这一目标。

此外，Marlo Henrique 建议将 SharedArray 与 k6 结合使用。

medium.com

与 Playwright 一起实现代码覆盖率

Sayed Afnan Khazi 告诉我们他们如何在一个重要的项目设置中寻找一种方法来获得 Playwright 测试覆盖率，以及 playwright-coverage 库如何实现这一目标。

说到这里，Bhavik Gandhi 编写了一份关于如何使用 Playwright 拦截和修改网络响应的有用指南。

medium.com

从 Cypress.env 迁移到 cy.env 和 Cypress.expose 方法

Cypress v16 中发生了重大变化，Gleb Bahmutov 展示了如何准备以新的方式处理环境变量和机密。

此外，Jennifer Shehane 刚刚宣布了另一项改进，允许您修复因找不到规范文件而导致的 Cypress CI 故障。

glebbahmutov.com

并且...

“质量太高了...” 🤣

感谢您的阅读！

如果您喜欢本新闻稿并发现它有助于成为更好的测试人员，请考虑与其他人分享。

大卫·迪洛维奇

<小时>

此 RSS 提要发布于 https://softwaretestingweekly.com/。 您还可以订阅 通过电子邮件。

item.title: 【Software Testing Weekly - Issue 304 - Mar 3rd 2026】软件测试周刊 - 第 304 期 - 2026 年 3 月 3 日
item.description:
【

COMMENT

Welcome to the 304th issue!

Big news! Meta announces a new testing method — Just-in‑Time Tests (JiTTests).

It's a new way of unit testing at scale thanks to the LLM-powered mutation technique.

The idea is simple — let AI generate lots of unit tests to catch possible failures, and then delete them.

Here's a 17-page research paper describing in detail why and how it works.

Also, Ayyaz Zafar summarises it: Meta's JiTTests: How AI Writes 22,000 Tests, Finds 4x More Bugs, Then Deletes Them All.

Happy (just-in-time) testing! 🙂

Dawid Dylowicz

Sponsored Link

These three BrowserStack AI Agents are changing how website QA works

BrowserStack's Website Scanner now has agents that don't just flag problems, they investigate them. Catch hidden accessibility gaps, get code-level fix guidance straight from your DOM, and reduce visual review time by 3x. Three agents, one scan, and fewer questions when it's time to ship. Scan your website for free!

browserstack.com

NEWS

Achieving Continuous Quality: Building a Culture That Lives and Breathes Value

Want to create a culture of quality in your team? Stuart Day shares how to go beyond mere testing activities and build a strong quality mindset and practice.

Similarly, Joshua Bihun describes what it takes to move towards Quality Assistance: Reframing QA From Gatekeeper to Coach.

medium.com

Development Got 10x Faster. Testing Didn't.

Hürkan Tuna raises a valid concern — it's becoming increasingly easier to build software faster, but proper verification and tracking are harder to scale. You can read more about it in the second part and the third part.

And, as Arik Aharoni points out, this creates a need for a new role — Test Critic: Navigating the AI Coverage Tsunami.

medium.com

Epistemic Testing: Chapter 4 — Who Tests the Tester?

Testing isn't just code — it's a mirror of your assumptions. Masoud Bahrami explores how doubt, curiosity, and bias influence every test you write.

On that note, Matthew Sullivan outlines why Software is treacherous and how we can reclaim purpose and influence in testing.

masoudbahrami.com

What AI Does in My QA & Automation Workflow

Curious where AI actually supports daily QA work? Todd Conner breaks down how he uses it across planning, scripting and debugging, and where human judgment is still best.

At the same time, Stuart Thomas advises to Forget coverage; focus on risk.

medium.com

What is software quality?

That's a million-dollar question! And for a good reason, as the answer depends on context. Ady Stokes makes a great attempt at providing possible answers from various people and sources.

Similarly, Stuart Day emphasises Why the Language We Use About Quality Matters More Than We Think.

ministryoftesting.com

AUTOMATION

Automation Testing, The Hidden Cost of `data-testid` & the Business Impact

In this series of five articles, Vivek Munde brings up several points on how to correctly identify web UI elements for test automation. You can also read parts two, three, four and five.

medium.com

How we Engineered an AI Agent That Writes, Compiles, Executes, and Ships E2E Tests

If you're looking for a detailed description of how to build an agentic AI solution for auto-generating test automation, Shrey Vats has something for you. Find out more in the second, third and fourth parts.

Additionally, Vera Marczenko explains how to effectively Let AI Write Your Assertions — Then Validate Them With Your Own Patterns.

medium.com

Page Object Model Is Dead: Here's What Replaced It

A bold statement from Pramod Dutta, but there are some arguments supporting it, including how the Screenplay and App Action patterns can replace it.

On that note, I'd also like to highlight A Field Guide to Not Overengineering Test Automation by Irfan Mujagić.

medium.com

The Testing Pyramid: Everybody Knows It, Nobody Follows It

Serhii Smetanskyi lists common pitfalls when trying to follow the test pyramid and explains what to do at each layer to make it work.

Moreover, Okan Yurt explains why Coverage ≠ Quality: Rethinking Unit Tests in the Age of AI.

linkedin.com

Sponsored Link

AI in testing is solving the wrong problem

If AI isn't helping your testing, maybe your approach is already broken. Discover why new tools can undermine testing outcomes, and how optimizing for coverage instead of business risk can backfire fast. If you are using AI in testing, this is an essential read. Check it out

tricentis.com

TOOLS

Advanced Playwright Authentication: A Multi-Role Fixture for Scalable E2E Testing

Testing web app flows with authentication is often challenging. But there's a way to make it much easier, and Sajith Dilshan shows how to achieve that with Playwright.

Furthermore, Faizan Shaikh provides a few tips on proper Playwright Error Handling Explained: Common Errors, Strict Mode, and Best Practices.

medium.com

Clean API tests with Cypress aliases

Bart Vanherck demonstrates a helpful solution to make Cypress API tests much more concise and easier to write.

Similarly, Mohammad Faisal Khatri wrote a helpful guide on How to test POST API Requests with Playwright TypeScript.

thetestingpirate.be

Giving AI Eyes and Hands — Which Browser MCP Actually Works

Wondering which browser MCP fits your automation tasks? Andrii Cheparskyi ran a hands-on comparison across Playwright MCP, Chrome DevTools MCP and Agent Browser, measuring how they behave under test.

medium.com

How a Learning Project Became Our Modern Mobile Test Framework

Raymond Saba and Breno Bittencourt walk through their shift from a legacy setup to a modular Appium 2/3 solution with plugins, parallel threads and shared ownership.

What's more, Anthony Dawson demonstrates how Appium MCP Unlocks New Methodologies in Mobile Automation.

trivago.com

JavaScript Web Test Automation Framework Rankings in JavaScript Rising Stars 2025

Curious which test tools were the most trending on GitHub in 2025? Each year, there's a slight change, and there should be no surprise at the new contenders given the rise of AI-first tools. By Courtney Zhan.

substack.com

VIDEOS

Playwright MCP + LM Studio: Your Private AI Test Agent — No Rate Limits, No Cloud — JUST FREE!

In another great example, Karthik KK shows how to pair Playwright MCP with LM Studio to set up a local test agent without paying a dollar.

youtube.com

AND...

95% pass rate... 😂

Thanks for reading!

If you enjoy this newsletter and find it helpful in becoming a better tester, please consider sharing it with others.

Dawid Dylowicz

This RSS feed is published on https://softwaretestingweekly.com/. You can also subscribe via email.

】

评论

欢迎来到第304期！

大新闻！ Meta 宣布了一种新的测试方法 - 即时测试 (JiTTests)。

得益于 LLM 支持的突变技术，这是一种大规模单元测试的新方法。

这个想法很简单——让 AI 生成大量单元测试来捕获可能的故障，然后删除它们。

这是一篇 17 页的研究论文，详细描述了它的工作原理和原理。

此外，Ayyaz Zafar 对其进行了总结：Meta 的 JiTTests：AI 如何编写 22,000 个测试，发现 4 倍以上的错误，然后将其全部删除。

快乐（及时）测试！ 🙂

大卫·迪洛维奇

赞助商链接

这三个 BrowserStack AI 代理正在改变网站 QA 的工作方式

BrowserStack 的网站扫描程序现在拥有的代理不仅可以标记问题，还可以调查问题。发现隐藏的可访问性差距，直接从 DOM 获取代码级修复指导，并将视觉审查时间减少 3 倍。三个代理，一次扫描，发货时问题更少。 免费扫描您的网站！

browserstack.com

新闻

实现持续的质量：建立一种有生命力和价值的文化

想要在您的团队中营造优质文化吗？ Stuart Day 分享了如何超越单纯的测试活动并建立强大的质量思维和实践。

同样，Joshua Bihun 描述了如何实现质量协助：重新构建从把关人到教练的 QA。

medium.com

开发速度加快了 10 倍。测试没有。

Hürkan Tuna 提出了一个合理的担忧——更快地构建软件变得越来越容易，但适当的验证和跟踪却很难扩展。您可以在第二部分和第三部分中阅读更多相关信息。

而且，正如 Arik Aharoni 指出的那样，这需要一个新角色 - 测试评论家：应对 AI 覆盖海啸。

medium.com

认知测试：第 4 章 — 谁来测试测试人员？

测试不仅仅是代码——它是你的假设的镜子。 Masoud Bahrami 探讨怀疑、好奇心和偏见如何影响您编写的每项测试。

就此而言，Matthew Sullivan 概述了为什么软件是危险的以及我们如何在测试中重新找回目的和影响力。

masoudbahrami.com

AI 在我的 QA 和自动化工作流程中的作用

想知道人工智能在哪里实际支持日常 QA 工作？ Todd Conner 详细介绍了他如何在规划、脚本编写和调试中使用它，以及在哪些方面人类判断仍然是最好的。

与此同时，Stuart Thomas 建议忘记报道；关注风险。

medium.com

什么是软件质量？

这是一个价值百万美元的问题！这是有充分理由的，因为答案取决于上下文。 Ady Stokes 做出了巨大的尝试，从不同的人和来源提供了可能的答案。

同样，Stuart Day 强调为什么我们使用的关于质量的语言比我们想象的更重要。

ministryoftesting.com

自动化

自动化测试、“data-testid”的隐藏成本以及业务影响

在这个由五篇文章组成的系列中，Vivek Munde 提出了有关如何正确识别测试自动化的 Web UI 元素的几点观点。您还可以阅读两部分、三、四和五部分。

medium.com

我们如何设计一个可编写、编译、执行和发送 E2E 测试的 AI 代理

如果您正在寻找有关如何构建用于自动生成测试自动化的代理 AI 解决方案的详细说明，Shrey Vats 可以为您提供帮助。在第二个中了解更多信息， 第三和第四部分。

此外，Vera Marczenko 解释了如何有效地让 AI 编写您的断言 - 然后用您自己的模式验证它们。

medium.com

页面对象模型已死：取代它的是它

Pramod Dutta 的大胆声明，但有一些论据支持它，包括剧本和应用操作模式如何取代它。

就此而言，我还想重点介绍 不要过度设计测试自动化的现场指南，作者是 Irfan Mujagić。

medium.com

测试金字塔：每个人都知道，但没有人遵循

Serhii Smetanskyi 列出了尝试遵循测试金字塔时的常见陷阱，并解释了在每一层要做什么才能使其发挥作用。

此外，Okan Yurt 解释了为什么覆盖率≠质量：重新思考人工智能时代的单元测试。

linkedin.com

赞助商链接

测试中的人工智能正在解决错误的问题

如果人工智能无法帮助您进行测试，也许您的方法已经失效了。了解为什么新工具会破坏测试结果，以及优化覆盖范围而不是业务风险如何会迅速适得其反。如果您在测试中使用人工智能，那么这是一本必读的书。 查看

tricentis.com

工具

高级剧作家身份验证：用于可扩展 E2E 测试的多角色装置

使用身份验证测试 Web 应用程序流程通常具有挑战性。但有一种方法可以让它变得更容易，Sajith Dilshan 展示了如何通过 Playwright 实现这一目标。

此外，Faizan Shaikh 提供了一些有关正确剧作家错误处理解释：常见错误、严格模式和最佳实践的提示。

medium.com

使用 Cypress 别名进行干净的 API 测试

巴特·范赫克 演示了一个有用的解决方案，使 Cypress API 测试更加简洁且更易于编写。

同样，Mohammad Faisal Khatri 编写了一份关于如何使用 Playwright TypeScript 测试 POST API 请求的有用指南。

thetestingpirate.be

为 AI 提供眼睛和双手 — 哪种浏览器 MCP 真正有效

想知道哪种浏览器 MCP 适合您的自动化任务？ Andrii Cheparskyi 对 Playwright MCP、Chrome DevTools MCP 和 Agent Browser 进行了实际比较，测量了它们在测试下的表现。

medium.com

学习项目如何成为我们的现代移动测试框架

Raymond Saba 和 Breno Bittencourt 演示他们从传统设置到模块化 Appium 2/3 解决方案（具有插件、并行线程和共享）的转变所有权。

此外，Anthony Dawson 演示了 Appium MCP 如何解锁移动自动化的新方法。

trivago.com

JavaScript Rising Stars 2025 中的 JavaScript Web 测试自动化框架排名

想知道 2025 年 GitHub 上哪些测试工具最热门？每年都会有轻微的变化，鉴于人工智能优先工具的兴起，新的竞争者应该不足为奇。作者：Courtney Zhan。

substack.com

视频

Playwright MCP + LM Studio：您的私人 AI 测试代理 — 无速率限制，无云 — 免费！

在另一个很好的示例中，Karthik KK 展示了如何将 Playwright MCP 与 LM Studio 配对，无需支付一美元即可设置本地测试代理。

youtube.com

并且...

95% 通过率... 😂

感谢您的阅读！

如果您喜欢本新闻稿并发现它有助于成为更好的测试人员，请考虑与其他人分享。

大卫·迪洛维奇

<小时>

此 RSS 提要发布于 https://softwaretestingweekly.com/。 您还可以订阅 通过电子邮件。

item.title: 【Software Testing Weekly - Issue 303 - Feb 23rd 2026】软件测试周刊 - 第 303 期 - 2026 年 2 月 23 日
item.description:
【

COMMENT

Welcome to the 303rd issue!

Remember Playwright MCP? There's something better now.

Microsoft released Playwright CLI — a more token-efficient interface for coding AI agents.

I'm sure you want to learn more, so here are some great resources:

  • Step-by-Step End-to-End Automation Using Microsoft Playwright CLI by Kailash Pathak,
  • playwright-cli: First Impressions and Key Takeaways by Andrew Goldis.

Happy testing! 🙂

Dawid Dylowicz

Sponsored Link

The 2026 State of AI in Software Testing

AI is now part of most QA workflows, but outcomes vary widely. BrowserStack's 2026 AI in Testing report is based on a global survey of more than 250 software testing leaders and examines how AI is being used in practice, where teams are seeing real gains, and how QA roles are beginning to shift as adoption grows. Download the report!

browserstack.com

NEWS

A Quality Czar Won't Fix Your System

A thought-provoking article by Alan Page on Microsoft's new Engineering Quality hire argues that shaping systems matters more to quality than just adding a quality‑focused role.

Similarly, Maria Kedemo raised that issue in a recent post — What's in a Title? Nothing. And Everything.

substack.com

AI Won't Fix Your Testing Strategy

"AI is an accelerant. And accelerants don't care whether the thing they're speeding up is good or bad." — Neil Duggan

This is seconded by Maaret Pyhäjärvi's experiment of Benchmarking results — Human, Human with AI, AI with Human and where we land.

And finally, it would be wise to start with understanding the Practical Principles for Agentic QA shared by Manu Cohen-Yashar.

medium.com

How We Release the Spotify App: A Look Under the Hood (Part 2)

Releasing an app used by hundreds of millions of users is not an easy feat. So it's great to learn more about how Spotify approaches it, thanks to Jacob Vesterlund and Katie Walker's detailed overview.

What's more, Russell Morley wrote about Mobile App Release Trains — When They Work and When They Don't.

atspotify.com

Measuring QA Success: Beyond Test Counts and Bug Reports

What quality metrics are worth measuring? Probably not the ones that you may think of, as Joshua Bihun points out.

Moreover, George Ukkuru concisely points out Why "Zero Bugs" Is the Wrong Goal in QA.

medium.com

Quality at speed

You might have heard this term many times. But does it really work? Maaike Brinkhof shares a sobering point of view on that.

Also, James Bach says So, You "10x'd" Your Work… and outlines why AI can't speed up everything, especially in testing.

maaikebrinkhof.nl

What Makes a Good Tester?

It's hard to disagree with Ryan Craven's detailed overview. It may also help you to realise the difference between The Testing Mindset vs Development Mindset.

Which leads me to another good piece by Maaike Brinkhof explaining What developers get wrong about testing.

substack.com

AUTOMATION

Lessons I Wish I Knew When I Started in Test Automation

Whether you're starting your test automation journey or you're already on it, David Ingraham makes several good points on what to be mindful of.

Also, Kristijan Stefanoski shares Why Code Coverage Still Matters (And How We're Making It Faster for JavaScript).

medium.com

The Test Pyramid Doesn't Fit Everyone

Good reasoning by Kristijan Stefanoski that the test pyramid (or other shape) should be applied according to the system under test.

Furthermore, Leonardo explains: Which Tests Actually Add More Value in Backend Development?

stefanoski.me

AAAAA Testing: How to make tests AI-friendly

You might be familiar with the Arrange, Act, Assert pattern, but what do the additional letters stand for? Fedor Novikov shares an interesting concept.

Moreover, Thelma Ugonna raises points about Coding with AI: Three Lessons from Testing, One from Architecture.

medium.com

Tools Aren't Enough: Scaling AI Adoption for Engineering Teams

How does your AI-assisted coding journey look so far? Angie Jones shares the impact of the AI Champions program that they run in their company.

Similarly, Jean-Christophe Jamet describes what it takes to go From AI helpers to a new "QA" in the team.

angiejones.tech

Sponsored Link

What if release-week didn't mean late nights?

When testing peaks, execution becomes the bottleneck. Lynqa solves this by automatically executing your Xray manual test cases as written, inside Jira, then syncing traceable, step-by-step results back to Xray. Absorb crunch periods without rewriting tests or maintaining scripts. Install Lynqa (10 free credits) — Learn more.

smartesting.com

TOOLS

Building a Scalable Automation Framework with Playwright + TypeScript: Parts 8–12

Once again, Rajesh Yemul delivers a continuation of a detailed series on building a Playwright test framework. This part is about Waiting, Logging, and Observability, while the next ones are about:

  • Part 9: Step-Level Reporting, Failure Diagnostics, and Execution Visibility,
  • Part 10: Owning the Reporting Surface,
  • Part 11: Timestamped Runs and Deterministic Artifacts,
  • Part 12: Allure, Semantics, and Cross-Run Intelligence.

medium.com

n8n for QA: Automate the Boring Stuff You Keep Doing Manually

n8n is a powerful AI workflow automation tool and Serhii Smetanskyi explains the potential use cases for testers.

Additionally, Brad DerManouelian tells us to Stop Renting AI — Bring Your Own LLM to Test Case Generation.

linkedin.com

StageMask: A Visual Approach to Masking Dynamic Content in Playwright Tests

Doing visual testing with Playwright? You might like Irfan Mujagić's tool for visually marking which parts you want to ignore, called StageMask.

Moreover, Sajith Dilshan describes the difference between Fixture vs Lazy Object Creation in Playwright: Avoiding Hidden Performance Traps.

thegreenreport.blog

"The Magnificent Seven" for Code Quality

If you're wondering what open-source solutions you can use to improve code quality, Federico Kereki has got you covered.

Also, Sébastien Boursault shares why I Actually Enjoy Writing Java Tests Now That I've Learned These Tips.

medium.com

Which API testing tool are you using?

Someone asked this question on Reddit, and the testing community has provided multiple answers. Looks like the days when Postman was the king are long gone!

And if you're leaning towards a programmatic solution, Mohammad Faisal Khatri wrote a continuation of Data Driven API Testing in Java with Rest-Assured and TestNG: Part 5.

reddit.com

VIDEOS

Agentic AI for Quality Engineering — Software Development and Testing

A very inspiring discussion between Alan Richardson and Dragan Spiridonov, who builds open-source agentic solutions for testing, e.g. the Agentic QE Framework.

youtube.com

Claude Code + Ollama: FREE Local AI Coding FOREVER

Want to use AI but don't want to pay for it? In this 15-minute recording, Karthik KK demonstrates how to set up Claude Code with an open-source model locally.

youtube.com

AND...

Now, that's a fix... 🤣

Thanks for reading!

If you enjoy this newsletter and find it helpful in becoming a better tester, please consider sharing it with others.

Dawid Dylowicz

This RSS feed is published on https://softwaretestingweekly.com/. You can also subscribe via email.

】

评论

欢迎来到第303期！

还记得剧作家MCP吗？现在有更好的东西了。

微软发布了Playwright CLI——一个用于编码人工智能代理的更令牌高效的界面。

我确信您想了解更多信息，因此这里有一些很棒的资源：

  • 使用 Microsoft Playwright CLI 逐步实现端到端自动化，作者：Kailash Pathak，
  • playwright-cli：第一印象和关键要点，作者：Andrew Goldis。

测试愉快！ 🙂

大卫·迪洛维奇

赞助商链接

2026 年软件测试中的人工智能状况

人工智能现在已成为大多数 QA 工作流程的一部分，但结果差异很大。 BrowserStack 的 2026 年 AI 测试报告基于对 250 多名软件测试领导者的全球调查，并研究了 AI 在实践中的使用方式、团队在哪些方面看到了真正的收益，以及随着采用率的增长，QA 角色如何开始转变。 下载报告！

browserstack.com

新闻

质量沙皇不会修复您的系统

Alan Page 发表了一篇关于 Microsoft 新聘请的工程质量人员的发人深省的文章，认为塑造系统对质量的影响比仅仅增加一个以质量为中心的角色更重要。

同样，Maria Kedemo 在最近的一篇文章中提出了这个问题 - 标题中有什么？没有什么。以及一切。

substack.com

人工智能不会修复您的测试策略

<块引用>

“人工智能是一种加速剂。加速剂并不关心它们加速的事物是好还是坏。” — Neil Duggan

Maaret Pyhäjärvi 的实验基准测试结果 - 人类、人类与人工智能、人工智能与人类以及我们的着陆点对此表示支持。

最后，明智的做法是从了解 Manu Cohen-Yashar 分享的代理 QA 实用原则开始。

medium.com

我们如何发布 Spotify 应用程序：深入了解（第 2 部分）

发布一款被数亿用户使用的应用程序并不是一件容易的事。因此，感谢 Jacob Vesterlund 和 Katie Walker 的详细概述，了解 Spotify 如何实现这一目标是件好事。

此外，Russell Morley 撰写了有关移动应用发布训练 — 何时有效和何时无效的文章。

atspotify.com

衡量 QA 成功：超越测试计数和错误报告

哪些质量指标值得衡量？正如 Joshua Bihun 指出的那样，可能不是您想到的。

此外，George Ukkuru 简洁地指出了为什么“零错误”是 QA 中的错误目标。

medium.com

质量与速度

您可能多次听说过这个词。但这真的有效吗？ Maaike Brinkhof 对此发表了发人深省的观点。

此外，James Bach 说所以，你的工作“10 倍”了…… 并概述了为什么人工智能无法加快一切速度，尤其是在测试中。

maaikebrinkhof.nl

什么才是优秀的测试人员？

很难不同意 Ryan Craven 的详细概述。它还可以帮助您认识测试心态与开发心态之间的区别。

这让我看到了 Maaike Brinkhof 的另一篇好文章，解释了开发人员在测试中犯了什么错误。

substack.com

自动化

我希望在开始测试自动化时知道的教训

无论您是正在开始测试自动化之旅还是已经开始，David Ingraham 都针对需要注意的事项提出了一些很好的观点。

此外，Kristijan Stefanoski 分享了为什么 Code 覆盖率仍然很重要（以及我们如何使 JavaScript 速度更快）。

medium.com

测试金字塔并不适合所有人

Kristijan Stefanoski 提出了很好的推理，即应该根据被测系统应用测试金字塔（或其他形状）。

此外，Leonardo 解释道：哪些测试实际上在后端开发中增加了更多价值？

stefanoski.me

AAAAA 测试：如何使测试对 AI 友好

您可能熟悉 Arrange、Act、Assert 模式，但是附加字母代表什么？ Fedor Novikov 分享了一个有趣的概念。

此外，Thelma Ugonna 提出了关于使用人工智能进行编码：测试的三个教训，架构的一个教训的观点。

medium.com

工具还不够：扩大工程团队的人工智能采用

到目前为止，您的人工智能辅助编码之旅进展如何？ Angie Jones 分享了他们在公司运行的 AI Champion 计划的影响。

同样，Jean-Christophe Jamet 描述了如何从 AI 助手转变为团队中的新“QA”。

angiejones.tech

赞助商链接

如果发布周并不意味着深夜怎么办？

当测试达到峰值时，执行就成为瓶颈。 Lynqa 解决了这个问题，方法是在 Jira 内自动执行编写的 Xray 手动测试用例，然后将可跟踪的分步结果同步回 Xray。吸收关键时期而无需重写测试或维护脚本。 安装 Lynqa（10 个免费积分） — 了解更多。

smarttesting.com

工具

使用 Playwright + TypeScript 构建可扩展的自动化框架：第 8-12 部分

Rajesh Yemul 再次提供有关构建Playwright 测试框架的详细系列的延续电子工作。这部分是关于等待、日志记录和可观察性，而接下来的部分是关于：

  • 第 9 部分：步骤级报告、故障诊断和执行可见性，
  • 第 10 部分：拥有报告界面，
  • 第 11 部分：带时间戳的运行和确定性工件，
  • 第 12 部分：魅力、语义和跨运行智能。

medium.com

n8n for QA：自动化您一直手动执行的无聊工作

n8n 是一款功能强大的 AI 工作流程自动化工具，Serhii Smetanskyi 为测试人员解释了潜在的用例。

此外，Brad DerManouelian 告诉我们停止租用 AI — 带上你自己的 LLM 来生成测试用例。

linkedin.com

StageMask：在剧作家测试中屏蔽动态内容的视觉方法

与 Playwright 一起进行视觉测试吗？您可能喜欢 Irfan Mujagić 的工具，用于直观地标记要忽略的部分，称为 StageMask。

此外，Sajith Dilshan 描述了Playwright 中的固定对象与惰性对象创建之间的区别：避免隐藏的性能陷阱。

thegreenreport.blog

代码质量的“七巨头”

如果您想知道可以使用哪些开源解决方案来提高代码质量，Federico Kereki 可以帮助您。

此外，Sébastien Boursault 分享了为什么既然我已经学会了这些技巧，我实际上很喜欢编写 Java 测试。

medium.com

您使用的是哪种 API 测试工具？

有人在 Reddit 上问了这个问题，测试社区提供了多个答案。看来邮递员为王的日子已经一去不复返了！

如果您倾向于程序化解决方案，Mohammad Faisal Khatri 撰写了使用 Rest-Assured 和 TestNG 在 Java 中进行数据驱动 API 测试：第 5 部分。

reddit.com

视频

用于质量工程的代理 AI - 软件开发和测试

Alan Richardson 和 Dragan Spiridonov 之间进行了一次非常鼓舞人心的讨论，他们构建了用于测试的开源代理解决方案，例如代理量化宽松框架。

youtube.com

Claude Code + Ollama：永远免费的本地人工智能编码

想要使用人工智能但又不想付费？在这段 15 分钟的录制中，Karthik KK 演示了如何在本地使用开源模型设置 Claude Code。

youtube.com

并且...

现在，解决了... 🤣

感谢您的阅读！

如果您喜欢本新闻稿并发现它有助于成为更好的测试人员，请考虑与其他人分享。

大卫·迪洛维奇

<小时>

此 RSS 提要发布于 https://softwaretestingweekly.com/。 您还可以订阅 通过电子邮件。

item.title: 【Software Testing Weekly - Issue 302 - Feb 15th 2026】软件测试周刊 - 第 302 期 - 2026 年 2 月 15 日
item.description:
【

COMMENT

Welcome to the 302nd issue!

Wondering how to leverage AI to future-proof your career as a tester?

Today, I'd like to highlight two intriguing resources to help you understand it better:

  • AI in Testing — What Should I Learn to Stay Future-Ready?
  • AI and Testing: Personal Marketability by Jeff Nyman

Happy (AI) testing! 🙂

Dawid Dylowicz

Sponsored Link

We made Appium obsolete (Meta, Microsoft & Amazon agree)

Maestro: the only test framework that doesn't make you want to flip tables. As one user put it: "just a few commands and boom, you're good to go.". Human-readable flows anyone can debug. No setup hell, no flaky tests. Run tests free with the CLI or visually build them in Maestro Studio Desktop — also free → Download now.

maestro.dev

NEWS

AI in Testing #1: From Hype to Habits (How Testing Wins in 2026)

In this four-part overview, Andrii Cheparskyi shares some good, practical advice on how to effectively use AI in testing. You can read more about ChatGPT & Claude Features, the AI IDEs and MCP for testers.

medium.com

Everyone is NOT Responsible for Quality

The notion of everyone being responsible for quality has been widely popularised in agile. But James Bach challenges it, recommending replacing it with personal responsibility and clear roles.

Moreover, Julia Kocbek shares interesting thoughts on How to solve dilemmas as QA.

satisfice.com

How I Transitioned from Traditional QA to AI-Assisted QA (And What I Actually Use Daily)

AI is here to help us and we need to learn how to use it effectively. Medhani Ranaweera points out several areas in which she found it helpful.

And if you're one of the late adopters, you may resonate with Dennis Martinez's experience of going through My Five Stages of AI Grief.

medium.com

Quality Debt: The Hidden Cost That Outgrows Technical Debt

Arik Aharoni explains how testing gaps accumulate over time and can hurt in the long term. It's a good reminder to regularly review test debt and risks.

In that context, Jitesh Gosai tells us how we can leverage Learning from failure: spikes, POCs, prototypes and MVPs.

testuff.com

When you outsource testing to the AI, you lose the ability to understand your system

Well said and reasoned, Patrick Prill! While AI can help us with some tasks, we shouldn't let it completely loose and detach too much from what it's doing.

Similarly, Katja Obring wrote about the importance of treating Review as analysis, not authority, when working with AI.

And all this makes people wonder — Is manual QA making a comeback due to AI?

wordpress.com

AUTOMATION

From 380 to 700+ Tests: How We Built an Autonomous QA Team with Claude Code

Shrinath Rao shares a high-level overview of their agentic AI approach to test automation. I only wish we could see some examples of that, too!

At the same time, Bart Vanherck points out Why AI won't save your Flaky Tests.

openobserve.ai

How I learned to trust synthetic data in performance testing

Sometimes, test data is as important as the test design itself. Sudhakar Reddy Narra gives several tips on how to approach synthetic test data generation and what to be mindful of.

ministryoftesting.com

Testing Error Boundaries by Breaking Contracts, Not Code

One common gap in automation is partial failures. Irfan Mujagić shows examples of simulating contract violations to check whether the rest of the app still works.

thegreenreport.blog

The case for running E2E tests against stable branches

Want less noise from flaky suites? Rodrigo Alves Costa explains why you should focus on fast unit and integration feedback early and run broader system tests only after merging into the main branch.

However, Amrinder Singh Mallhi worryingly notices The Lost Art of Dry Running in today's software development.

medium.com

Sponsored Link

Are You Paying the "Specialty Penalty"? Read the State of Testing™ Report to Find Out

The State of Testing™ report by PractiTest explores current and emerging trends shaping QA, from teams navigating the AI Paradox to how modern tool adoption influences salaries, roles, and expectations. Gain data-backed insight into how testing organizations are evolving and where to focus next. Read the full report.

practitest.com

TOOLS

Appium 3.x Manifesto: Installation, Driver Management, and Architectural Compatibility

Appium has recently upgraded to version 3+ and Hakan Tektaş describes all the changes and requirements for migration.

medium.com

How I Used Rovo AI in Jira to Level Up Software Testing

Now that Jira and Confluence have Rovo AI built in, there's an opportunity to use it for improving software testing processes. Parul Kaushik explains the potential use cases.

Also, wondering whether it's worth Using Claude code to understand code before testing?

medium.com

Implementing MCP load tests with Grafana k6

Hrvoje Milković describes how they approached and what they learned from load testing an MCP server using k6.

And if you want to learn more, Millan Kaul gives a quick recap — What is MCP?

infobip.com

Using agent skills to write Playwright tests

There's a lot we can learn from Tim Lolkema's approach to setting up AI agent workflows for generating Playwright tests.

substack.com

Why Test Case Management Systems Still Matter

One of the topics that often yields different opinions within the testing community is test case management. Is it needed? Here's what Brad DerManouelian thinks about it.

testplanit.com

VIDEOS

What Is Testing? A Conversation with James Bach and Michael Bolton

I don't think an introduction is required for this one. Just tune in and enjoy a fine conversation about the essence of testing between the testing legends — James Bach and Michael Bolton — invited by Alex Khvastovich.

youtube.com

AND...

The release ride... 😂

Thanks for reading!

If you enjoy this newsletter and find it helpful in becoming a better tester, please consider sharing it with others.

Dawid Dylowicz

This RSS feed is published on https://softwaretestingweekly.com/. You can also subscribe via email.

】

评论

欢迎来到第302期！

想知道如何利用人工智能来保证您作为测试人员的职业生涯的未来？

今天，我想重点介绍两个有趣的资源，以帮助您更好地理解它：

  • 人工智能测试 — 我应该学习什么才能为未来做好准备？
  • 人工智能和测试：个人市场能力 作者：Jeff Nyman

祝（AI）测试愉快！ 🙂

大卫·迪洛维奇

赞助商链接

我们让 Appium 过时（Meta、微软和亚马逊都同意）

Maestro：唯一不会让您想要翻转表格的测试框架。正如一位用户所说：“只需几个命令，然后就可以开始了。”。任何人都可以调试的人类可读流程。没有设置地狱，没有不稳定的测试。使用 CLI 免费运行测试或在 Maestro Studio Desktop 中直观地构建它们 — 也是免费的 → 立即下载。

maestro.dev

新闻

人工智能在测试中的应用 #1：从炒作到习惯（测试如何在 2026 年获胜）

在这个由四部分组成的概述中，Andrii Cheparskyi 分享了一些关于如何在测试中有效使用 AI 的实用建议。您可以详细了解 ChatGPT 和 Claude 功能、AI IDE 以及面向测试人员的MCP。

medium.com

每个人都不对质量负责

每个人都对质量负责的理念在敏捷中得到了广泛的普及。但詹姆斯·巴赫对此提出质疑，建议用个人责任和明确的角色来取代它。

此外，Julia Kocbek 分享了关于如何解决 QA 困境的有趣想法。

satisfice.com

我如何从传统 QA 过渡到 AI 辅助 QA（以及我每天实际使用的工具）

人工智能是来帮助我们的，我们需要学习如何有效地使用它。 Medhani Ranaweera 指出了她认为该指南很有帮助的几个领域。

如果您是较晚采用者之一，您可能会引起共鸣Dennis Martinez 经历我的人工智能悲伤的五个阶段的经验。

medium.com

质量债务：超越技术债务的隐性成本

Arik Aharoni 解释了测试差距如何随着时间的推移而积累，并从长远来看会造成伤害。这是定期审查测试债务和风险的一个很好的提醒。

在此背景下，Jitesh Gosai 告诉我们如何利用从失败中学习：峰值、POC、原型和 MVP。

testuff.com

当你将测试外包给人工智能时，你就失去了理解系统的能力

说得好，很有道理，帕特里克·普里尔！虽然人工智能可以帮助我们完成一些任务，但我们不应该让它完全脱离它正在做的事情。

类似地，Katja Obring 写道，在使用人工智能时，将将评论视为分析而非权威的重要性。

这一切都让人想知道 - 手动 QA 是否会因人工智能而卷土重来？

wordpress.com

自动化

从 380 次到 700 多次测试：我们如何使用 Claude Code 建立自治 QA 团队

Shrinath Rao 分享了他们用于测试自动化的代理 AI 方法的高级概述。我只希望我们也能看到一些例子！

同时，Bart Vanherck 指出为什么 AI 不会保存您的 Flaky 测试。

openobserve.ai

我如何学会在性能测试中信任合成数据

有时，测试数据与测试设计本身一样重要。 Sudhakar Reddy Narra 提供了一些关于如何生成综合测试数据以及需要注意的事项的提示。

ministryoftesting.com

通过破坏契约而不是代码来测试错误边界

自动化中的一个常见差距是部分故障。 Irfan Mujagić 显示模拟示例检查合同违规情况，以检查应用程序的其余部分是否仍然有效。

thegreenreport.blog

针对稳定分支运行端到端测试的案例

想要减少脆弱套房的噪音吗？ Rodrigo Alves Costa 解释了为什么您应该尽早关注快速单元和集成反馈，并仅在合并到主分支后才运行更广泛的系统测试。

然而，Amrinder Singh Mallhi 令人担忧地注意到当今软件开发中的失落的空运行艺术。

medium.com

赞助商链接

您是否正在支付“专业处罚”？阅读 State of Testing™ 报告来了解详情

PractiTest 的《测试现状™》报告探讨了塑造 QA 的当前和新兴趋势，从应对 AI 悖论的团队到现代工具的采用如何影响薪资、角色和期望。获得数据支持的洞察，了解测试组织如何发展以及下一步的重点。 阅读完整报告。

practitest.com

工具

Appium 3.x 宣言：安装、驱动程序管理和架构兼容性

Appium 最近已升级到版本 3+，Hakan Tektaş 描述了迁移的所有更改和要求。

medium.com

我如何在 Jira 中使用 Rovo AI 来提升软件测试水平

现在 Jira 和 Confluence 内置了 Rovo AI，因此有机会使用它来改进软件测试流程。 Parul Kaushik 解释了潜在的用例。

另外，想知道是否值得在测试之前使用 Claude 代码来理解代码？

medium.com

使用 Grafana k6 实施 MCP 负载测试

Hrvoje Milković 描述了他们的方法以及他们从使用 k6 对 MCP 服务器进行负载测试中学到的知识。

如果您想了解更多信息，Millan Kaul 提供了快速回顾p — 什么是 MCP？

infobip.com

使用代理技能编写 Playwright 测试

我们可以从 Tim Lolkema 设置 AI 代理工作流程以生成 Playwright 测试的方法中学到很多东西。

substack.com

为什么测试用例管理系统仍然很重要

测试用例管理是测试社区中经常产生不同意见的主题之一。需要吗？以下是Brad DerManouelian对此的看法。

testplanit.com

视频

什么是测试？与詹姆斯·巴赫和迈克尔·博尔顿的对话

我认为不需要对此进行介绍。请收听并享受由 Alex Khvastovich 邀请的测试传奇人物 - James Bach 和 Michael Bolton 之间关于测试本质的精彩对话。

youtube.com

并且...

发布之旅... 😂

感谢您的阅读！

如果您喜欢本新闻稿并发现它有助于成为更好的测试人员，请考虑与其他人分享。

大卫·迪洛维奇

<小时>

此 RSS 提要发布于 https://softwaretestingweekly.com/。 您还可以订阅 通过电子邮件。

item.title: 【Software Testing Weekly - Issue 301 - Feb 3rd 2026】软件测试周刊 - 第 301 期 - 2026 年 2 月 3 日
item.description:
【

COMMENT

Welcome to the 301st issue!

Two years ago, we learned how Reddit approaches the eternal fight with flaky tests.

Today, I'm happy to share with you the second part of that journey, delivered by Abinodh Thomas:

From Fragile to Agile Part II: The Sequence-based Dynamic Test Quarantine System.

There's some good inspiration there.

Happy testing! 🙂

Dawid Dylowicz

Sponsored Link

Lynqa: Execute your manual tests with AI

Lynqa executes your manual test cases as-is from Xray and provides step-by-step, traceable results automatically synced back to Xray. It helps handle regression peaks, saves time and execution costs, and lets QA focus on higher-value testing. Try it with 10 free credits.

smartesting.com

NEWS

Am I the only one that finds QA easier than Dev?

I regularly see people asking about switching between dev and QA, and this thread offers some good insights into what it takes.

And it's not only me — John Caleb Pananganan has also noticed this trend of Developers Wanting to Move to QA, and QA Wanting to Move to Development, including the follow-up second part.

reddit.com

AI did not break testing

Is our profession really stuck? Katja Obring makes a few good points on why we should share and apply our testing knowledge, especially now.

Similarly, Amalie Shi shares interesting thoughts after attending a webinar about How to Coexist with AI as a Tester.

kato-coaching.com

How to sustainably elevate your Quality Assurance in practice

If you want to improve your QA processes, Viviane Hennecke and Ralf Kleinhietpass share their approach focused on putting people first, adjusting roles and leveraging AI for support.

Similarly, Tamar Baratashvili has a few solid points on how Testers Help Products Become What They Were Meant to Be.

medium.com

Releases should be smooth, fast, and boring

I really like this statement by Elizabeth Zagroba. As a vision, that's what we should aim for.

Furthermore, Michael Tomara wonders about Calculating the QA: Not So Simple in search for the perfect dev-to-QA ratio.

elizabethzagroba.com

Quality Control Layer: The Rise of Testing?

How do you keep quality when AI builds most of the software? Yanir Taflev reflects on changing responsibilities of testers to keep adding value.

Moreover, Rodrigo Alves Costa brings up an important point on Why Manual Testing is More Relevant in the Age of AI.

medium.com

AUTOMATION

Automation test branching and environments preparation

In order to enable collaboration between testers, Muhammad Ahyar Ibrahim Aldebaran came up with a way of handling tests per environment using sandbox, staging and master branches.

medium.com

Chain of Responsibility for Test Setup: Make Test Data Read Like a Spec

Vera Marczenko gives plenty of practical advice on how to improve the "arrange" part in tests. It's followed by another article about AI-Generated Test Data: Powerful, Dangerous, or Both?.

medium.com

I stopped writing E2E tests manually. I manage an AI agent instead

Bold claim, Antony Fuentes! But as the saying goes, there might be a method to this madness, as explained here in detail.

instawork.com

From Fragile Locators to Unbreakable Tests: Semantic Similarity with Sentence Embeddings

Suryakanta Mohapatra shares a technical deep dive into how they achieved auto-locator healing using the all-MiniLM-L6-v2 sentence transformer model.

medium.com

TOOLS

Building a Scalable Automation Framework with Playwright + TypeScript: Part 7

Rajesh Yemul continues a great series of articles showing practical code examples to create a full-fledged test automation framework with Playwright. This part focuses on assertions.

Moreover, Sajith Dilshan shows a few tricks to speed up Playwright tests by Mastering Non-Linear Execution with Async/Await Patterns.

medium.com

Checkmate — Stop Writing Test Scripts, Start Describing Tests

Kulanthaivelu Sankaran describes a handy, open-source AI agent solution for generating tests using natural language commands called Checkmate.

medium.com

Cypress.Promise.all() and cy.mapChain(): Two Hidden Gems for Cypress You Won't Let Go Once You Try Them

If you're looking to improve your Cypress tests, Sebastian Clavijo Suero demonstrates commands that can help you handle async patterns.

dev.to

Data Driven API Testing in Java with Rest-Assured and TestNG
